test: Single-box empirical probes (1 RTX 5090, layers 28-31) resolving the three gating questions for M2.5 Phase 3 (StaticKV+CUDA-graph) and Phase 5 (concurrency). Cheap decisive GPU dev — no ring needed.
box: 1x RTX 5090 (sm_120), torch 2.10+cu128, vLLM 0.23 NVFP4 cutlass FusedMoE

phase3a_static_kv_hardware.verdict: BIT-IDENTICAL to cat on real hardware
phase3a_static_kv_hardware.evidence: same prefill+verify+rollback sequence: cat hash c333820d665d7629 == static hash c333820d665d7629 (through SDPA-flash, deterministic per-GPU)
phase3a_static_kv_hardware.memory: static pre-reserves +168 MB/layer at M25_KV_MAXLEN=40960 (+0.66 GB for 4 layers; ~2.2 GB for a full 13-layer stage) vs cat's grow-as-needed
phase3a_static_kv_hardware.implication: M25_STATIC_KV is a safe drop-in; the fixed-MAXLEN memory cost is the tradeoff for fixed addresses

phase3b_cuda_graph_feasibility.verdict: FEASIBLE + BIT-EQUIVALENT + 3.40x — the gating risk (NVFP4 MoE graph-safety) is RESOLVED
phase3b_cuda_graph_feasibility.capture: CUDA-graph capture of run_block (static KV + NVFP4 cutlass MoE + SDPA, fixed K+1 shape) SUCCEEDED — the MoE and SDPA are graph-safe on sm_120
phase3b_cuda_graph_feasibility.bit_equivalence: graph-vs-eager max|diff| = 0.000e+00 (the mandatory verify-path gate passes)
phase3b_cuda_graph_feasibility.speedup: per-block eager 7.65ms -> graph 2.25ms = 3.40x on 4 layers (confirms per-stage GPU is launch-overhead-bound, the documented lever)
phase3b_cuda_graph_feasibility.remaining: production impl: graph the varying-start_pos decode/verify path via a static cp index buffer + a bucketed ADDITIVE mask (causal_lower_right mis-aligns under bucketed reads), opt-in M25_CUDA_GRAPH, per-bucket recapture. Feasibility + bit-exactness now proven.

phase5_moe_invariance.verdict: NON-INVARIANT to token count — lossless BATCHED MoE is OFF the table (same class as the mxfp4 issue)
phase5_moe_invariance.evidence: token-0 MLP output: B=2 identical (diff 0), but B=4/8/16 diverge by a REPRODUCIBLE 1.953e-3 (rel 3.36e-3) — kernel grouping/padding, not FP noise
phase5_moe_invariance.implication: Phase 5 concurrency must use PER-STREAM MoE (attention still batches, ~1.24x on the old engine) or keep RING_LOCK + a request queue. A batched MoE would break spec-decode/receipt bit-exactness, so it's not acceptable for the 'provably honest' pitch.

cost: ~$0.30 (one box ~20 min) vs ~$11.76 for the 5-box ring pass — single-box is the right tool for single-stage empirical questions
